# System Qt6 in CI is not built with -fsanitize=thread, so TSan cannot see
# the synchronization in QCoreApplication::postEvent / event-queue mutex.
# This makes every queued signal/slot delivery look like a race -- either on
# the QMetaCallEvent itself (caught by called_from_lib) or on the by-value
# arguments copied into our slot via the moc-generated dispatcher (caught by
# the qt_static_metacall pattern, which only matches the moc shim).
called_from_lib:libQt6Core.so
race:qt_static_metacall
race:QtPrivate::QMetaTypeForType

# Qt6 on Linux uses glib's main loop by default, which wakes worker threads
# via an eventfd. Synchronization happens through the kernel fd, which TSan
# doesn't model; glib itself isn't instrumented either.
called_from_lib:libglib-2.0.so
